---
title: "PI Predictability Scorecard"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
import json
from pathlib import Path
from IPython.display import HTML, display
import plotly.graph_objects as go

_candidates = [Path("data/pi-predictability.json"), Path("../data/pi-predictability.json")]
data_file = next((p for p in _candidates if p.exists()), None)
if data_file is None:
    raise FileNotFoundError(
        "data/pi-predictability.json not found. "
        "Run: python NceGitLab.py --report pi-predictability"
    )

d     = json.loads(data_file.read_text())
group = d["group"]
pis   = d["pis"]
rows  = d["rows"]
port  = d["portfolio_row"]
```

```{python}
HTML(f"""
<p>
  <strong>Report Date:</strong> {d['report_date']} &nbsp;|&nbsp;
  <strong>Group:</strong> <a href="{group['url']}" target="_blank">{group['name']}</a>
</p>
<p>Percentage of committed Features and Capabilities delivered in each PI.
Target ≥ 80%. Consistently at 100% may indicate sandbagging; below 60% signals a
systemic problem.</p>
""")
```

## 🗺️ Predictability Heatmap

Each cell shows delivery % for one ART × PI combination. Green ≥ 80%, yellow 60–79%, red < 60%.
Future PIs (not yet started) and cells with no data are shown as blank.

```{python}
if not pis:
    display(HTML("<p><em>No PI-committed Features or Capabilities found.</em></p>"))
else:
    # Build lookup: art_name → {piid → cell}
    art_names = [row["art_name"] for row in rows]
    art_pi_lookup = {
        row["art_name"]: {cell["piid"]: cell for cell in row["cells"]}
        for row in rows
    }

    # Only include PIs where at least one ART has a numeric pct (past/current)
    plotable_pis = [
        p for p in pis
        if any(
            art_pi_lookup.get(a, {}).get(p, {}).get("pct") is not None
            for a in art_names
        )
    ]

    if not plotable_pis:
        display(HTML("<p><em>No completed or in-progress PIs to plot yet.</em></p>"))
    else:
        z_vals   = []
        txt_vals = []
        hover    = []

        for art_name in art_names:
            z_row = []
            t_row = []
            h_row = []
            for piid in plotable_pis:
                cell = art_pi_lookup[art_name].get(piid, {})
                pct  = cell.get("pct")
                z_row.append(pct)
                t_row.append(f"{pct}%" if pct is not None else "")
                h_row.append(
                    f"<b>{art_name}</b><br>"
                    f"PI: {piid}<br>"
                    f"{cell.get('icon', '—')} {cell.get('label', '—')}<br>"
                    f"Delivered: {cell.get('closed', '—')} / {cell.get('total', '—')}"
                )
            z_vals.append(z_row)
            txt_vals.append(t_row)
            hover.append(h_row)

        # Portfolio row
        port_z   = []
        port_txt = []
        port_h   = []
        for piid in plotable_pis:
            port_cells = {c["piid"]: c for c in (port or [])}
            cell = port_cells.get(piid, {})
            pct  = cell.get("pct")
            port_z.append(pct)
            port_txt.append(f"{pct}%" if pct is not None else "")
            port_h.append(
                f"<b>Portfolio Total</b><br>"
                f"PI: {piid}<br>"
                f"{cell.get('icon', '—')} {cell.get('label', '—')}<br>"
                f"Delivered: {cell.get('closed', '—')} / {cell.get('total', '—')}"
            )
        z_vals.append(port_z)
        txt_vals.append(port_txt)
        hover.append(port_h)
        y_labels = art_names + ["Portfolio Total"]

        # Custom colorscale: red 0% → yellow 60% → green 80% → dark-green 100%
        colorscale = [
            [0.0,  "#ef4444"],
            [0.6,  "#f59e0b"],
            [0.8,  "#86efac"],
            [1.0,  "#16a34a"],
        ]

        fig = go.Figure(go.Heatmap(
            z=z_vals,
            x=plotable_pis,
            y=y_labels,
            text=txt_vals,
            texttemplate="%{text}",
            hovertext=hover,
            hoverinfo="text",
            colorscale=colorscale,
            zmin=0,
            zmax=100,
            showscale=True,
            colorbar=dict(
                title="Pred%",
                tickvals=[0, 60, 80, 100],
                ticktext=["0%", "60%", "80%", "100%"],
            ),
            xgap=2,
            ygap=2,
        ))

        fig.update_layout(
            height=max(250, len(y_labels) * 52 + 120),
            margin=dict(l=200, r=80, t=30, b=100),
            xaxis=dict(tickangle=-35, side="bottom"),
            plot_bgcolor="#f9fafb",
            paper_bgcolor="white",
            font=dict(size=12),
        )
        fig.show()
```

## 📊 Predictability Trend by ART

Bar chart showing delivery % per PI for each ART. 80% reference line (green), 60% caution line (yellow).
Hover for committed/delivered counts.

```{python}
if not pis or not rows:
    display(HTML("<p><em>No data to plot.</em></p>"))
else:
    ART_COLORS = [
        "#3b82f6", "#10b981", "#f59e0b", "#10b981",
        "#ef4444", "#06b6d4", "#f97316", "#6366f1",
    ]

    art_pi_lookup = {
        row["art_name"]: {cell["piid"]: cell for cell in row["cells"]}
        for row in rows
    }

    plotable_pis = [
        p for p in pis
        if any(
            art_pi_lookup.get(row["art_name"], {}).get(p, {}).get("pct") is not None
            for row in rows
        )
    ]

    if not plotable_pis:
        display(HTML("<p><em>No completed or in-progress PIs to plot.</em></p>"))
    else:
        fig = go.Figure()

        for i, row in enumerate(rows):
            art_name = row["art_name"]
            art_data = art_pi_lookup[art_name]
            color = ART_COLORS[i % len(ART_COLORS)]

            y_vals = []
            hovers = []
            for piid in plotable_pis:
                cell = art_data.get(piid, {})
                pct  = cell.get("pct")
                y_vals.append(pct)
                hovers.append(
                    f"<b>{art_name}</b><br>"
                    f"PI: {piid}<br>"
                    f"{cell.get('icon', '—')} {cell.get('label', '—')}<br>"
                    f"Delivered: {cell.get('closed', '—')} / {cell.get('total', '—')}"
                )

            fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
                name=art_name,
                x=plotable_pis,
                y=y_vals,
                marker_color=color,
                opacity=0.82,
                hovertext=hovers,
                hoverinfo="text",
            ))

        # Portfolio line overlay
        if port:
            port_cells = {c["piid"]: c for c in port}
            port_y = [port_cells.get(p, {}).get("pct") for p in plotable_pis]
            port_h = [
                f"<b>Portfolio Total</b><br>"
                f"PI: {p}<br>"
                f"{port_cells.get(p, {}).get('icon', '—')} {port_cells.get(p, {}).get('label', '—')}"
                for p in plotable_pis
            ]
            fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
                name="Portfolio",
                x=plotable_pis,
                y=port_y,
                mode="lines+markers",
                line=dict(color="#111827", width=2, dash="dot"),
                marker=dict(size=8, color="#111827"),
                hovertext=port_h,
                hoverinfo="text",
            ))

        fig.add_hline(y=80, line_dash="dot", line_color="#16a34a", line_width=1.5,
                      annotation_text="80% target", annotation_position="right",
                      annotation_font_color="#16a34a", annotation_font_size=10)
        fig.add_hline(y=60, line_dash="dot", line_color="#f59e0b", line_width=1.5,
                      annotation_text="60% caution", annotation_position="right",
                      annotation_font_color="#f59e0b", annotation_font_size=10)

        fig.update_layout(
            barmode="group",
            yaxis=dict(title="Predictability %", range=[0, 110]),
            height=420,
            margin=dict(l=60, r=80, t=30, b=80),
            xaxis=dict(tickangle=-35),
            plot_bgcolor="#f9fafb",
            paper_bgcolor="white",
            font=dict(size=12),
            legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
        )
        fig.show()
```

## Scorecard Table

```{python}
if not pis:
    display(HTML("<p><em>No PI-committed Features or Capabilities found. Ensure epics carry <code>PIID::</code> labels.</em></p>"))
else:
    # Header
    pi_headers = "".join(f"<th>{p}</th>" for p in pis)
    html  = f'<table class="table table-sm table-bordered"><thead><tr><th>ART</th>{pi_headers}</tr></thead><tbody>'

    for row in rows:
        art_link = f'<a href="{row["art_url"]}" target="_blank">{row["art_name"]}</a>' if row["art_url"] else row["art_name"]
        cells = "".join(f'<td>{c["icon"]} {c["label"]}</td>' for c in row["cells"])
        html += f"<tr><td><strong>{art_link}</strong></td>{cells}</tr>"

    if port:
        port_cells = "".join(f'<td>{c["icon"]} {c["label"]}</td>' for c in port)
        html += f"<tr><td><strong>Portfolio Total</strong></td>{port_cells}</tr>"

    html += "</tbody></table>"
    display(HTML(html))
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Legend

**Predictability %** = closed Features + Capabilities ÷ total committed to PI × 100

| Icon | Range | Meaning |
|------|-------|---------|
| ✅ | ≥ 80% | On target — team reliably delivers commitments |
| ⚠️ | 60–79% | Watch — delivery shortfall, investigate root cause |
| ❌ | < 60% | At risk — systemic delivery problem, escalate |
| 🟡 | Current PI | In progress — final score not yet determined |
| 🔵 | Future PI | Not yet started — shows planned commitment count |

> **Sandbagging signal:** an ART consistently at 100% across multiple past PIs may be
> under-committing. Healthy predictability is typically 80–90%.
:::
